identity.name: Shaurya Verma
identity.headline: CSE undergrad building agentic AI for Indian public problems

samples: GLOAMING — The Board That Plays Back. A browser co-op board game where the board is the antagonist: a rising Dread tide, a hidden traitor, a hunting Stalker, an AI narrator + hand-authored fallback.
Fast Radio Burst DM anomaly detection for dark matter halo fingerprinting along lines of sight.
Cosmic void shape statistics as a dark energy equation-of-state probe — w0 and wa constraints.
Dark matter subhalo gap detection in stellar streams via KDE density analysis.
Star Wars Galactic Battle Simulator — React + Vite app with live SWAPI data, 3-round animated battles, holographic dark UI.

entries.title: B.Tech Computer Science & Engineering — Thapar Institute of Engineering & Technology, Patiala
GLOAMING — The Board That Plays Back
entries.summary: Browser co-op board game where the board is the antagonist: a rising Dread tide, a hidden traitor, a hunting Stalker, an AI narrator with a hand-authored fallback.

bullets.text: Built a browser co-op board game where the board itself is the antagonist — Dread tide, hidden traitor, hunting Stalker — using boardgame.io, Vite, and serverless functions
Designed an AI narrator with a hand-authored fallback so the full game runs with zero API keys — the LLM amplifies, never gates
Shipped live to Vercel with the AI layer keyless-degradable end to end

entries.title: DARYA — Village-Level Flood Intelligence for Rupnagar
entries.summary: Dam, gauge, and IMD data fused into per-village Punjabi alerts delivered through a trusted human relay, with a replayable evaluation timeline of the 2025 Punjab floods.

bullets.text: Fusing dam-release, river-gauge, and IMD forecast data into village-level flood risk for Rupnagar district, delivered as Punjabi-language alerts through a trusted human relay
Building a replayable evaluation timeline of the 2025 Punjab floods so every alert threshold is backtested against a real disaster, not a synthetic benchmark

entries.title: MUNSHI — AI Exam Scribe for Blind Students
entries.summary: Zero-network, browser-first exam scribe whose trust mechanism is a tamper-evident hash chain.

bullets.text: Building a zero-network, browser-first AI exam scribe for blind students — nothing leaves the machine during an exam
Designing a tamper-evident hash chain as the trust mechanism, so invigilators can verify the session log cryptographically instead of trusting the software

entries.title: KATHA VAULT — Voice AI Archive of Elder Folklore
entries.summary: A voice agent that records elders telling folk stories in native dialects, with an elder-tuned turn-taking engine and a geo-tagged story atlas.

bullets.text: Building a voice AI agent that archives elder folklore in native dialects, with an elder-tuned turn-taking engine — extended patience windows and verbal backchannels instead of interruptions
Designing a geo-tagged story atlas so every recorded tale is pinned to the village that told it

entries.title: YOJANA SETU — Voice-First Welfare-Scheme Eligibility Agent
entries.summary: "LLM talks, rules decide": deterministic rules-JSON verdicts, a live entitlement counter, and a pre-filled print packet, with Punjabi ASR via Groq Whisper large-v3.

bullets.text: Building a voice-first welfare-scheme eligibility agent on the principle 'LLM talks, rules decide' — every verdict comes from deterministic rules-JSON, never from the model
Wiring Punjabi speech recognition through Groq Whisper large-v3 with a live entitlement counter and a pre-filled print packet as the output

entries.title: SUTRADHAR — Cinema Intelligence Platform
entries.summary: A market-permission graph between films, a band-based box-office verdict engine, and Natya-Shastra-grounded craft guidance for Indian storytellers.

bullets.text: Building a cinema intelligence platform: a market-permission graph between films and a band-based box-office verdict engine where every verdict shows its comparable films
Grounding craft guidance in Natya-Shastra rasa theory, with every note backed by named films and real performances from the corpus

entries.title: Braillix — Math OCR on Raspberry Pi for Assistive Use
entries.summary: Math OCR running on a Raspberry Pi for assistive reading.

bullets.text: Building math OCR on a Raspberry Pi for assistive use — on-device recognition, no cloud dependency
